# Model Selection and Methodology

## Preprocessing Pipeline

```{python}
#| label: preprocessing

# Handling missing values (imputation strategy)
# Encoding categorical variables
# Scaling / normalization
# Train-test split strategy
```

## Candidate Models

<!--
For each model, briefly justify why it is suitable for this task.
-->

### Logistic Regression

<!--
- Baseline model, highly interpretable
- L1 (Lasso) regularization for built-in feature selection
- Suitable for both large and small datasets
-->

### Random Forest

<!--
- Handles non-linear relationships and feature interactions
- Built-in feature importance
- Robust to outliers
-->

### XGBoost

<!--
- State-of-the-art for tabular data
- Handles class imbalance via scale_pos_weight
- SHAP integration for interpretability
-->

### Support Vector Machine

<!--
- Effective in high-dimensional spaces
- Useful if class separation is non-linear (RBF kernel)
-->

## Evaluation Strategy

<!--
- Stratified k-fold cross-validation (k=5 or k=10)
- Metrics: Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1, AUC-ROC
- Why AUC and Recall matter more than accuracy in clinical context
- Handling class imbalance: SMOTE, class weights, or undersampling
-->

```{python}
#| label: model-training

# Model training and cross-validation code
```

## Hyperparameter Tuning

```{python}
#| label: hyperparameter-tuning

# Grid search or Optuna setup
```

## Biomarker Subanalysis

<!--
Separate modeling pipeline for the smaller biomarker dataset (~384 patients).
- More conservative approach (logistic regression, small RF)
- Repeated stratified CV to account for small sample
- Compare: model with cognitive features only vs. cognitive + biomarkers
-->

```{python}
#| label: biomarker-model

# Biomarker-specific pipeline
```
